Description (Level 1): Executes the ongoing capture, maintenance, and correction of property, ownership, and party records to ensure the Land Tax register is accurate, authoritative, and fit for all downstream valuation and taxation activities.
Executing Capabilities (L2 Execution):
Customer Identity & Relationship Management, 
Ownership Structure & Trust Management, 
Property Data Acquisition & Quality
Property Information Services
Description (Level 2): Initiates changes to property ownership by capturing factual transfer information required to update authoritative ownership records.
Defines the legal persons (individuals, companies, trusts) necessary to create valid ownership and taxation relationships.
Creates and maintains the property entity that underpins valuation, treatment, and assessment activities.
Defines who owns what interest in a property, which is a prerequisite for valuation and liability determination.
Ensures ongoing accuracy and integrity of core records that support all downstream Land Tax processes.

Description (Level 1): Executes the determination and maintenance of statutory land values, including general, supplementary, and Commissioner’s valuations, ensuring that authoritative land values are established and kept current for Land Tax purposes.
Description (Level 2): Establishes baseline land values through scheduled valuation cycles that apply broadly across properties.
Applies event-driven valuation updates triggered by changes such as subdivision, consolidation, or corrected data.
Determines valuation components based on land use or occupancy factors where required by legislation.
Provides statutory valuations determined directly by the Commissioner where standard valuation pathways are insufficient.

Description (Level 1): Executes the application of Land Tax legislation to determine how land and owners are treated, including exemptions, concessions, groupings, and surcharge eligibility, prior to calculating any financial liability.
Executing Capabilities (L2 Execution):
Exemption Management
Land Grouping Administration
Absentee Owner Tax Treatment
Build‑to‑Rent Registration
Description (Level 2): Determines whether land is exempt from Land Tax based on legislative eligibility criteria.
Trigger: If we decide to define level 3  a standradised process description is more approriate than BPM

Description (Level 2): Applies concessional treatment rules that alter how Land Tax legislation applies to eligible land.
Determines whether properties or owners must be treated as a group for Land Tax purposes.
Determines whether absentee owner surcharge provisions apply before financial calculation occurs.

Description (Level 1): Executes the review and resolution of objections, disputes, and post‑assessment changes, including valuation objections, assessment objections, and amendments, to ensure Land Tax outcomes remain correct and defensible.
Executing Capabilities (L2 Execution):
Valuation Dispute Resolution
Adjustment Management
Exemption Management
Case Management (Complex / Specialist)
Description (Level 2): Handles formal challenges to determined land values without performing valuation itself.
Handles objections to issued Land Tax assessments after liability has been formalised.
Reviews prior exemption decisions where challenged or reassessed.
Applies approved changes to assessments arising from objections or corrected information.

Description (Level 1): Executes the calculation of financial obligations arising from land ownership by applying statutory rates, surcharges, penalties, and interest to land values and treatment outcomes.
Executing Capabilities (L2 Execution):
Assessment Lifecycle Management
New Liability Identification
Absentee Owner Tax Treatment
Description (Level 2): Calculates the primary Land Tax amount by applying statutory rates and thresholds to land value and treatment outcomes.
Calculates surcharge amounts once absentee owner eligibility has been established.
Calculates penalty amounts arising from non-compliance or late actions.
Calculates statutory interest on overdue Land Tax liabilities.

Value Stream: Land Tax Obligations Formally Established & Communicated
Description (Level 1): Executes the creation, issue, amendment, and cancellation of Land Tax assessments and notices, converting calculated liability into a formally issued and legally enforceable obligation.
Executing Capabilities (L2 Execution):
Assessment Lifecycle Management
Demand Generation & Payment Processing
Adjustment Management
Special Levy Administration
Description (Level 2): Creates the formal Land Tax assessment record that establishes a legally enforceable obligation.

Value Stream: Land Tax Obligations Formally Established & Communicated
Description (Level 2): Issues official notices communicating the assessed Land Tax and payment requirements to taxpayers.

Value Stream: Land Tax Obligations Formally Established & Communicated
Description (Level 2): Formally reverses or nullifies assessments where required under legislation or error correction.

Value Stream: Land Tax Obligations Formally Established & Communicated
Description (Level 2): Handles non-standard or specialised assessments that still form part of the assessment lifecycle.

Description (Level 1): Executes the receipt, allocation, reconciliation, and correction of Land Tax payments, including refunds and suspense handling, to accurately settle assessed obligations.
Executing Capabilities (L2 Execution):
Demand Generation & Payment Processing
Payment Suspense & Refund Management
Description (Level 2): Accepts Land Tax payments from taxpayers through approved payment channels.
Allocates received payments to the correct accounts and liabilities.
Processes repayment of overpaid or incorrectly applied Land Tax amounts.
Manages unresolved or unmatched payments pending clarification or correction.

Description (Level 1): Executes monitoring and enforcement actions for unpaid Land Tax liabilities, including recovery processes and insolvency management, where voluntary compliance has not occurred.
Executing Capabilities (L2 Execution):
Debt Recovery & Enforcement
Case Management (Complex / Specialist)
Description (Level 2): Identifies Land Tax liabilities that remain unpaid beyond due dates.
Applies statutory recovery actions to collect outstanding Land Tax debts.
Manages Land Tax recovery where taxpayers enter insolvency arrangements.

Description (Level 1): Executes the provision of formal Land Tax certifications and assurances, such as Property Clearance Certificates, confirming Land Tax status to customers and third parties.
Executing Capabilities (L2 Execution):
Property Information Services
Assessment Quality Assurance
Description (Level 2): Provides legally required certification of Land Tax status for property transactions.
Provides formal confirmation of Land Tax compliance or liability status to customers or third parties.
